info.title: Gift
info.description: This API manages gift information and related entities such as gift splits, gift fundraisers, and soft credits.

get.description: Returns a list of custom fields for a gift.

parameters.name: gift_id
parameters.description: The immutable system record ID of the gift to retrieve custom fields for.

200.description: Returned when the operation succeeds. The response body contains a collection of custom field entities.

400.description: Returned when the specified gift ID in the request URL is not in the appropriate format.

403.description: Returned when the user does not have permission to view the custom fields.

404.description: Returned when the specified gift is not found.

get.description:
Returns a paginated list of gift custom fields.

The default sorting behavior is to list gift custom fields in ascending order based on when they were created.

However, the last_modified parameter overrides the default sorting behavior to list gift custom fields in ascending order based on when they were last modified. The last_modified parameter also adds the sort_token parameter to the next_link URL to ensure that gift custom fields are stably sorted and that order persists when changes occur while working through a paginated list.

If the last_modified and date_added parameters are both specified, the sorting behavior is to list gift custom fields based on when they were last modified.

Note: This endpoint returns data with an average latency of about 30 minutes. In addition, historic records have a default date_added timestamp of 1600-01-01 00:00:00.000 +00:00. The timestamp for newer records reflects when the records were added.

parameters.name: date_added
parameters.description: Represents a filter for gift custom fields created on or after the specified date. The filter respects time offsets from UTC per the ISO-8601 format: 2016-05-05T17:59:31.1600745-04:00.

parameters.name: last_modified
parameters.description: Represents a filter for gift custom fields modified on or after the specified date. The filter respects time offsets from UTC per the ISO-8601 format: 2016-05-05T17:59:31.1600745-04:00.

parameters.name: sort_token
parameters.description: Represents a token filter to provide the next stable-sorted list of gift custom fields. This will be provided on the next_link collection response property when last_modified or sort_token filters are specified on the request.

parameters.name: category
parameters.description: Represents a filter for custom field categories. Returns any custom fields that match the specified category. For example, category=Interests returns custom fields with the Interests category.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: Represents a filter for custom field values. Must be used in conjunction with the category filter. Returns any custom fields in the specified category that match any of the specified values. For example, category=Interests&value=Baseball&value=Tennis returns Interests custom fields with the Baseball or Tennis values. Filtering by fuzzy date values must be in one of the following formats: YYYYMMDD, YYYY, YYYYMM, or MMDD. Any four digit value that starts with 13 or higher will be considered a year. Any four digit value that starts with 01-12 will be considered to be in MMDD format.

parameters.name: gift_id
parameters.description: Represents a list of gift identifiers. Returns custom fields if any of the specified gift identifiers match any of the custom fields' associated gifts. For example, "gift_id=280&gift_id=1232" returns custom fields with either "280" or "1232" gift identifiers.

parameters.name: include_count
parameters.description: False to exclude the count of all records. Default is true.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Represents the number of records to return. The default is 500. The maximum is 5000.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Represents the number of records to skip. For use with pagination.

200.description: Returned when the operation succeeds. The response body contains a collection of custom field entities.

400.description: Returned when the specified request parameters are not in the appropriate format.

403.description: Returned when the user does not have permission to view the custom fields.

requestBody.description: An object that represents the custom field to create.

200.description: Returned when the operation successfully creates a custom field. The response body contains the ID of the new custom field.

400.description: Returned when the request body is not in the appropriate format.

403.description: Returned when the user does not have permission to create the custom field.

404.description: Returned when the specified gift is not found.

get.description: Returns a list of custom field categories.

200.description: Returned when the operation succeeds. The response body contains a collection of custom field categories.

403.description: Returned when the user does not have permission to view the custom field categories.

post.description: Creates a gift custom field category if it doesn't exist or retrieves the id of the existing category.

requestBody.description: An object that represents the custom field category to create.

200.description:
Returned when the operation successfully creates or finds a custom field category.
The response body contains the ID of the new custom field category.

400.description: Returned when the request body is not in the appropriate format.

403.description: Returned when the user does not have permission to create the custom field category.

get.description: Returns the list of custom field category values.

parameters.name: category_name
parameters.description: The name of the category to return values for.

200.description: Returned when the operation succeeds. The response body contains a collection of string custom field category values.

400.description: Returned when the specified request parameter is not in the appropriate format.

403.description: Returned when the user does not have permission to view the custom field category values.

parameters.name: custom_field_id
parameters.description: The immutable system record ID of the custom field to delete.

200.description: Returned when the operation succeeds and the custom field has been deleted.

400.description: Returned when the specified custom field ID in the request URL is not in the appropriate format.

403.description: Returned when the user does not have permission to delete the custom field.

parameters.name: custom_field_id
parameters.description: The immutable system record ID of the custom field to edit.

requestBody.description: An object that represents the properties of the custom field to edit.

example.comment: Sent to primary email and left voicemail

200.description: Returned when the operation successfully edits the custom field.

400.description: Returned when the specified custom field ID in the request URL or the specified request parameters are not in the appropriate format.

403.description: Returned when the user does not have permission to edit the custom field.

404.description: Returned when the specified custom field is not found.

parameters.name: gift_id
parameters.description: The immutable system record ID of the gift to retrieve information for.

200.description: Returned when the operation succeeds. The response body contains the specified gift entity.

400.description: Returned when the specified gift ID in the request URL is not in the appropriate format.

403.description: Returned when the user does not have permission to view the gift.

404.description: Returned when the specified gift is not found.

patch.description:
Edits a gift.
Posted gifts cannot be modified, except to change recurring gift status.

parameters.name: gift_id
parameters.description: The immutable system record ID of the gift to edit.

parameters.name: If-Match
parameters.description: ETag of the Entity. The value should match the current ETag header value from the gift GET request or it should be * for an unconditional update.

requestBody.description: An object that represents the properties of the gift to edit.

200.description: OK

400.description: Returned when the specified gift ID in the URL or the specified request body is not in the appropriate format.

403.description: Returned when the user does not have permission to edit the gift.

404.description: Returned when the specified gift is not found.

delete.description:
Deletes a gift. If a gift was added in database view or added through the API with the is_manual flag set to true, you can only delete it if it is a recurring gift, recurring gift payment, or pledge. You can delete gifts added via web view except for any recurring gift payment. Recurring gifts added via web view cannot be deleted if their current status is Active or Held. Pledge gifts added via web view or added through the API with the is_manual flag set to false cannot be deleted if their payment method is Credit card or Direct debit. Regardless of type, you can’t delete a gift if it has:
  • claimed gift aid
  • a payment with a live transaction ID
  • a GL distribution record
  • an adjustment
  • a matching gift
  • a pledge payment
  • a recurring gift payment
  • a write-off
  • receipt history
  • a receipt status of Receipted

parameters.name: gift_id
parameters.description: The immutable system record ID of the gift to delete.

200.description: Returned when the operation succeeds and the gift has been deleted.

400.description: Returned when the specified gift ID in the request URL is not in the appropriate format.

403.description: Returned when the user does not have permission to delete the gift.

409.description: Returned when the gift isn't eligible to be deleted.

get.description:
Returns a paginated list of gifts.

The default sorting behavior is to list gifts in ascending order based on the id.

However, the last_modified parameter overrides the default sorting behavior to list gifts in ascending order based on when they were last modified, and the date_added parameter overrides the default sorting behavior to list gifts in ascending order based on when they were created. The last_modified and date_added parameters also add the sort_token parameter to the next_link URL to ensure that gifts are stably sorted and that order persists when changes occur while working through a paginated list.

If the last_modified and date_added parameters are both specified, the sorting behavior is to list gifts based on when they were last modified.

Note: This endpoint returns data with an average latency of about 30 minutes.

parameters.name: date_added
parameters.description: Represents a filter for gifts created on or after the specified date. The filter respects time offsets from UTC per the ISO-8601 format: 2016-05-05T17:59:31.1600745-04:00.

parameters.name: last_modified
parameters.description: Represents a filter for gifts modified on or after the specified date. The filter respects time offsets from UTC per the ISO-8601 format: 2016-05-05T17:59:31.1600745-04:00.

parameters.name: sort_token
parameters.description: Represents a token filter to provide the next stable-sorted list of gifts. This will be provided on the next_link collection response property when last_modified or sort_token filters are specified on the request.

parameters.name: constituent_id
parameters.description: Represents a list of constituent identifiers. Returns gifts if any of the specified constituent identifiers match any of their constituents. For example, "constituent_id=280&constituent_id=1232" returns gifts with either "280" or "1232" constituent identifiers.

parameters.name: post_status
parameters.description: Represents a list of gift post statuses. Returns gifts if their post status matches any specified. For example, "post_status=DoNotPost&post_status=Posted" returns gifts that are marked either DoNotPost or Posted.

parameters.name: gift_type
parameters.description: Represents a list of gift types. Returns gifts if their type matches any specified. For example, "gift_type=MatchingGiftPledge&gift_type=RecurringGift" returns gifts of type MatchingGiftPledge or RecurringGift. Available values are Donation, GiftInKind, MatchingGiftPledge, MatchingGiftPayment, PlannedGift, Pledge, PledgePayment, RecurringGift, RecurringGiftPayment, Stock, SoldStock, and Other.

parameters.name: receipt_status
parameters.description: Represents a list of gift receipt statuses. Returns gifts if their receipt status matches any specified. For example, "receipt_status=DoNotReceipt&receipt_status=Receipted" returns gifts that are marked either DoNotReceipt or Receipted.

parameters.name: acknowledgement_status
parameters.description: Represents a list of gift acknowledgement statuses. Returns gifts if their acknowledgement status matches any specified. For example, "acknowledgement_status=DoNotAcknowledge&acknowledgement_status=Acknowledged" returns gifts that are marked either DoNotAcknowledge or Acknowledged.

parameters.name: campaign_id
parameters.description: Represents a list of campaign identifiers. Returns gifts if any of the specified campaign identifiers match any of their campaigns. For example, "campaign_id=280&campaign_id=1232" returns gifts with either "280" or "1232" campaign identifiers.

parameters.name: fund_id
parameters.description: Represents a list of fund identifiers. Returns gifts if any of the specified fund identifiers match any of their funds. For example, "fund_id=280&fund_id=1232" returns gifts with either "280" or "1232" fund identifiers.

parameters.name: appeal_id
parameters.description: Represents a list of appeal identifiers. Returns gifts if any of the specified appeal identifiers match any of their appeals. For example, "appeal_id=280&appeal_id=1232" returns gifts with either "280" or "1232" appeal identifiers.

parameters.name: start_gift_date
parameters.description: Represents a filter for gifts with a gift date on or after the specified date. If used with end_gift_date, returns gifts with gift dates between both values.

parameters.name: end_gift_date
parameters.description: Represents a filter for gifts with a gift date on or before the specified date. If used with start_gift_date, returns gifts with gift dates between both values.

parameters.name: start_gift_amount
parameters.description: Represents a filter for gifts with an amount greater than or equal to the specified amount. If used with end_gift_amount, returns gifts with amounts between both values.

parameters.name: end_gift_amount
parameters.description: Represents a filter for gifts with an amount less than or equal to the specified amount. If used with start_gift_amount, returns gifts with amounts between both values.

parameters.name: list_id
parameters.description: Defines a list identifier used to filter the set of gifts to those included in the specified list. If this value is set, other specified filters will be ignored.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description:
Represents a list of fields to sort the results by. Returns a list that sorts gifts based on the supplied fields. Results are in ascending order by default, and a '-' sign denotes descending order. For example, "sort=date_added,-date" sorts gifts by the "date_added" field in ascending order and then by the "gift date" field in descending order.
If only the date_modified field or only the date_added field is provided, then this adds the sort_token parameter to the next_link URL to ensure that gifts are stably sorted.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Represents the number of records to return. The default is 500. The maximum is 5000.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Represents the number of records to skip. For use with pagination.

200.description: Returned when the operation succeeds. The response body contains a collection of gift entities.

ETag.description: The entity tag of the object to be returned in an If-Match header to subject the object to a concurrency check.

post.description: Creates a document upload location and unique document identifier for physical attachments.

requestBody.description: An object that represents the document to upload.

403.description: Returned when the user does not have permission to upload the document.

parameters.name: receipt_id
parameters.description: The immutable system record ID of the gift receipt to edit. It uses the parent gift's ID as its value.

requestBody.description: An object that represents the properties of the gift receipt to edit.

200.description: OK

400.description: Returned when the specified receipt ID in the URL or the specified request body is not in the appropriate format.

parameters.name: acknowledgement_id

post.description: Adds one or more gifts to a specified gift batch in the web view.

parameters.name: batch_id
parameters.description: The immutable system record ID of the batch to which the gifts will be added.

requestBody.description: A collection of objects representing the gifts to add to the given batch.

200.description: Returned when the operation successfully adds the gifts to the batch. The response body contains a list of IDs and any exceptions that occurred when adding the gifts.

400.description: Returned when the specified request body is not in the appropriate format.

403.description: Returned when the user does not have permission to add gifts to the batch.

put.description: Edit the status of a recurring gift.

parameters.name: gift_id
parameters.description: The immutable system record ID of the recurring gift to update.

requestBody.description: The new status to apply to the recurring gift.

200.description: Returned when the operation succeeds.

400.description: Returned when the specified request parameters are not in the appropriate format.

403.description: Returned when the user doesn't have permission to edit gift.

404.description: Returned when the specified gift ID doesn't exist.

get.summary: Recurring gift eligibility for automated processing
get.description: Returns whether the recurring gift can be converted to an automated gift in the web view.

parameters.name: gift_id
parameters.description: The ID of the recurring gift.

200.description: Returned when the operation succeeds. The response body indicates if the specified recurring gift can be automated.

errors.exception_error_message: This gift can't be automated because its credit card information has not been stored.

400.description: Returned when the specified request parameters are not in the appropriate format.

403.description: Returned when the user doesn't have permission to view gift.

404.description: Returned when the specified gift is not found or is not a recurring gift.

post.description: Converts a manual recurring gift to an automated recurring gift in the web view.

parameters.name: gift_id
parameters.description: The immutable system record ID of the recurring gift to convert.

requestBody.description: The BBPS configuration ID to use to process the gift's payments.

200.description: Returned when the operation successfully converts the gift to an automated recurring gift.

400.description: Returned when the specified request parameters are not in the appropriate format, or when the recurring gift can't be automated.

403.description: Returned when the user doesn't have permissions to edit the gift.

404.description: Returned when the specified gift is not found or is not a recurring gift.

post.description:
Returns a paginated list of gifts.

The default sorting behavior is to list gifts in ascending order based on the id.

However, the last_modified parameter overrides the default sorting behavior to list gifts in ascending order based on when they were last modified, and the date_added parameter overrides the default sorting behavior to list gifts in ascending order based on when they were created. The last_modified and date_added parameters also add the sort_token parameter to the next_link URL to ensure that gifts are stably sorted and that order persists when changes occur while working through a paginated list.

If the last_modified and date_added parameters are both specified, the sorting behavior is to list gifts based on when they were last modified.

Note: This endpoint returns data with an average latency of about 30 minutes.

Currency.description: For consistency, currency is configured at the organization level. This ensures that all monetary amounts are consistent, regardless of where they are entered or viewed.

count.description: The number of items available for retrieval into the collection after applying any request parameters. The limit and offset parameters do not affect the count, but to facilitate paging, they may affect the number of items in the value result set.

next_link.description: For paginated responses, the URI for the next page of results.

value.description: The set of items included in the response. This may be a subset of the items in the collection.

ApiCollection_CustomFieldRead_.description: Fetching data all at once quickly becomes unmanageable. The collection entity helps by returning paginated chunks of large data sets. This entity includes helpful metadata and properties to paginate and iterate through the data.

id.description: The immutable system record ID of the custom field.

category.description: The custom field category. Available values are the entries in the Custom Field Categories table of the parent object.

comment.description: The comment on the custom field.

date.description: The date on the custom field. Uses ISO-8601 format:1969-11-21T10:29:43.

date_added.description: The date when the custom field was created. The date includes an offset from UTC in ISO-8601 format:1969-11-21T10:29:43-04:00.

date_modified.description: The date when the custom field was last modified. The date includes an offset from UTC in ISO-8601 format:1969-11-21T10:29:43-04:00.

parent_id.description: The parent object's immutable system record ID.

type.description: The type of data that the custom field represents. Available values are listed below.

value.description: The value of the custom field. The type property determines the format.

CustomFieldRead.description: While records provide many fields to track information, organizations often require additional details. To track this specialized information, use custom fields.
CustomFieldRead.x-additional-info:
Available values for the type of a custom field:

Type Description
Boolean Defines the custom field as a boolean value.
CodeTableEntry Defines the custom field as a string value that represents a code table entry description.
ConstituentId Defines the custom field as a constituent identifier value.
Currency Defines the custom field as a decimal value.
Date Defines the custom field as a date value in ISO-8601 format: 1969-11-21T10:29:43.
FuzzyDate Defines the custom field as a fuzzy date value.
Number Defines the custom field as an integer value.
Text Defines the custom field as a string value. Character limit: 255.

category.description: The custom field category. Available values are the entries in the Custom Field Categories table of the parent object.

comment.description: The comment on the custom field. Character limit: 50.

date.description: The date on the custom field. Uses ISO-8601 format:1969-11-21T10:29:43.

parent_id.description: The parent object's immutable system record ID.

value.description: The value of the custom field. The type property determines the format. Character limit depends on data type. Number types only support values greater than 0.

CustomFieldAdd.description: While records provide many fields to track information, organizations often require additional details. To track this specialized information, use custom fields.

id.description: The immutable system record ID of the gift.

acknowledgements.description: The set of acknowledgements associated with the gift.

batch_number.description: The batch number associated with this gift.

constituent_id.description: The immutable system record ID of the constituent associated with the gift.

date_added.description: The date when the gift was created. Includes an offset from UTC in ISO-8601 format: 1969-11-21T10:29:43-04:00.

date_modified.description: The date when the gift was last modified. Includes an offset from UTC in ISO-8601 format: 1969-11-21T10:29:43-04:00.

fundraisers.description: The set of fundraisers who receive credit for the gift.

gift_aid_qualification_status.description: This computed field determines the Gift Aid qualification status based on tax declaration information and the database format. Available values are: Qualified, NotQualified, and PartlyQualified. For the UK only.

gift_code.description: The gift code value associated with the gift.

gift_splits.description: The set of gift splits associated with the gift.

gift_status.description: The status of the gift. Available values are Active, Held, Terminated, Completed, and Cancelled.

is_anonymous.description: Indicates whether the gift is anonymous.

linked_gifts.description: The identifiers of other gifts that are linked to this gift.

lookup_id.description: The user-defined identifier for the gift.

post_date.description: The date that the gift was posted to general ledger. Uses ISO-8601 format: 1969-11-21T10:29:43.

post_status.description: The general ledger post status of the gift. Available values are Posted, NotPosted, and DoNotPost. When post_status is set to DoNotPost, post_date should be null. When it is set to NotPosted, post_date is required but remains editable. When it is set to Posted, post_date is required and is no longer editable.

receipts.description: The set of receipts associated with the gift.

reference.description: Notes to track special details about a gift such as the motivation behind it or a detailed description of a gift-in-kind.

soft_credits.description: The set of soft credits associated with the gift.

subtype.description: The subtype of the gift. Available values are the entries in the Gift Subtypes table.

type.description: The gift type. Available values are Donation, GiftInKind, Pledge, PledgePayment, RecurringGift, RecurringGiftPayment, Stock, SoldStock, Other, PlannedGift, MatchingGiftPledge, MatchingGiftPayment

GiftRead.description: Gifts are the primary goal of fundraising efforts. They come in many forms and have a lot of information associated with them to ensure that they are properly allocated and acknowledged.
GiftRead.x-additional-info:
Available values for the type of a gift:

Type Description
Amendment An amendment.
Donation A donation.
GiftInKind A gift-in-kind.
GeneralLedgerReversal A general ledger reversal.
MatchingGiftPledge A matching gift pledge.
MatchingGiftPayment A matching gift payment.
MatchingGiftWriteOff A matching gift write-off.
PlannedGift A planned gift.
Pledge A pledge.
PledgePayment A pledge payment.
PledgeWriteOff A pledge write off.
RecurringGift A recurring gift.
RecurringGiftPayment A recurring gift payment.
Stock A stock.
SoldStock A sold stock.
Other Another type of gift.

date.description: The date associated with the acknowledgement. Uses ISO-8601 format: 1969-11-21T10:29:43.

letter.description: The letter associated with the acknowledgement.

status.description: The status of the acknowledgement. Available values are: ACKNOWLEDGED, NEEDSACKNOWLEDGEMENT, and DONOTACKNOWLEDGE. When using the GET Gift List endpoint, the type string for NEEDSACKNOWLEDGEMENT returns as NotAcknowledged.

AcknowledgementRead.description: Acknowledgement letters foster relationships with donors and show appreciation for their contributions. It is important to keep track of the acknowledgement status of gifts to ensure that each one gets a well-deserved thank you.

constituent_id.description: The immutable constituent system record ID for the fundraiser associated with the gift.

GiftFundraiserRead.description: Fundraiser constituents interact with other constituents and prospects on behalf of your organization to cultivate relationships and request donations. When a constituent makes a donation, the fundraiser can receive credit to track performance and foster fundraising accomplishments.

id.description: The immutable system record ID of the gift split.

appeal_id.description: The immutable system record ID of the appeal associated with the gift split.

campaign_id.description: The immutable system record ID of the campaign associated with the gift split.

fund_id.description: The immutable system record ID of the fund associated with the gift split.

gift_aid_qualification_status.description: The gift aid qualification status of the gift split. Available values are: Qualified, and NotQualified. For the UK only.

package_id.description: The immutable system record ID of the package associated with the gift split.

GiftSplitRead.description: Not all gifts serve a single purpose. Gift splits track donor wishes and allow you to divide gifts among multiple campaigns, funds, and appeals.

account_token.description: The tokenized account information (e.g. credit card) from the external payment provider. Only applies to payment methods of CreditCard and DirectDebit.

bbps_configuration_id.description: The bbps configuration ID. Only applies to payment methods of CreditCard and DirectDebit.

bbps_transaction_id.description: The bbps transaction ID. Only applies to payment methods of CreditCard and DirectDebit.

check_number.description: The check number. Only applies to payment method of PersonalCheck.

checkout_transaction_id.description: The checkout transaction ID. Only applies to payment methods of CreditCard and DirectDebit.

payment_method.description: The payment method. Available values are listed below.

reference.description: The reference. Only applies to payment method of Other.

PaymentRead.description: Donors can use a variety of methods — such as Cash, Personal check, Credit card, and Direct debit — to pay for gifts.
PaymentRead.x-additional-info:
Available values for the payment_method of a payment:

Type Description
Cash Cash.
PersonalCheck A personal check.
CreditCard A credit card.
StandingOrder A standing order.
DirectDebit Direct debit.
Voucher A voucher.
Other Another method of payment.
PayPal PayPal
Venmo Venmo

Note: For the ListGifts endpoint, only payment_method is returned.

date.description: The date on the receipt. Uses ISO-8601 format: 1969-11-21T10:29:43.

status.description: The status of the receipt. Available values are: RECEIPTED, NEEDSRECEIPT, and DONOTRECEIPT.

ReceiptRead.description: To help donors track their giving for tax purposes, your organization should provide gift receipts.

end_date.description: Date the recurring gift should end. Uses ISO-8601 format: 1969-11-21T10:29:43.

frequency.description: Installment frequency of the recurring gift to view. Available values are WEEKLY, EVERY_TWO_WEEKS, EVERY_FOUR_WEEKS, MONTHLY, QUARTERLY, ANNUALLY.

start_date.description: Date the recurring gift should start. Uses ISO-8601 format: 1969-11-21T10:29:43.

next_transaction_date.description: The date of the next scheduled transaction. Only applies to Recurring Gifts and Pledges.

RecurringGiftScheduleRead.description: Defines a recurring gift schedule to view.

d.description: The day in the fuzzy date.

m.description: The month in the fuzzy date.

y.description: The year in the fuzzy date.

FuzzyDate.description: Fuzzy dates provide a versatile date type to create partial dates such as February 9 (with no year indicated).

id.description: The immutable system record ID of the soft credit.

constituent_id.description: The immutable system record ID of the constituent associated with the soft credit.

gift_id.description: The immutable system record ID of the gift associated with the soft credit.

SoftCreditRead.description: Soft credits allow you to recognize and track the indirect contributions of constituents such as spouses who influence the gifts of other constituents.

lookup_id.description: The user-defined identifier for the gift. Character limit: 50.

date.description: The gift date. Includes an offset from UTC in ISO-8601 format: 1969-11-21T10:29:43.

is_anonymous.description:
Indicates whether the gift is anonymous.
If no value is provided, the default anonymity of the donor will be used.

reference.description: Notes to track special details about a gift such as the motivation behind it or a detailed description of a gift-in-kind. Character limit: 255.

gift_status.description: The status of the gift. Available values are Active, Held, Terminated, Completed, and Cancelled. The status can only be changed for gifts of type pledge and recurring gift. The status cannot be changed from Terminated, Completed, or Cancelled to Active or Held.

gift_code.description: The gift code of the gift.

subtype.description: The subtype of the gift. Available values are the entries in the Gift Subtypes table.

GiftEdit.description:
An object that represents the gift to edit.
Gifts are the primary goal of fundraising efforts. They come in many forms and have a lot of information associated with them to ensure that they are properly allocated and acknowledged.

finder_number.description: Gets or sets the gift's finder number. The finder number cannot be negative.

mailing_id.description: Gets or sets the gift's mailing identifier.

marketing_source_code.description: Gets or sets the gift's marketing source code. Character limit: 255.

GiftMarketingDetailEdit.description: Represents marketing information for a gift.
GiftMarketingDetailEdit.x-additional-info:
Available values for the type of a gift:

Type Description
Amendment An amendment.
Donation A donation.
GiftInKind A gift-in-kind.
GeneralLedgerReversal A general ledger reversal.
MatchingGiftPledge A matching gift pledge.
MatchingGiftPayment A matching gift payment.
MatchingGiftWriteOff A matching gift write-off.
PlannedGift A planned gift.
Pledge A pledge.
PledgePayment A pledge payment.
PledgeWriteOff A pledge write off.
RecurringGift A recurring gift.
RecurringGiftPayment A recurring gift payment.
Stock A stock.
SoldStock A sold stock.
Other Another type of gift.

date_added.description: Represents a filter for gifts created on or after the specified date. The filter respects time offsets from UTC per the ISO-8601 format: 2016-05-05T17:59:31.1600745-04:00.

last_modified.description: Represents a filter for gifts modified on or after the specified date. The filter respects time offsets from UTC per the ISO-8601 format: 2016-05-05T17:59:31.1600745-04:00.

sort_token.description: Represents a token filter to provide the next stable-sorted list of gifts. This will be provided on the next_link collection response property when last_modified or sort_token filters are specified on the request.

constituent_id.description: Represents a list of constituent identifiers. Returns gifts if any of the specified constituent identifiers match any of their constituents. For example, "constituent_id=280&constituent_id=1232" returns gifts with either "280" or "1232" constituent identifiers.

post_status.description: Represents a list of gift post statuses. Returns gifts if their post status matches any specified. For example, "post_status=DoNotPost&post_status=Posted" returns gifts that are marked either DoNotPost or Posted.

gift_type.description: Represents a list of gift types. Returns gifts if their type matches any specified. For example, "gift_type=MatchingGiftPledge&gift_type=RecurringGift" returns gifts of type MatchingGiftPledge or RecurringGift. Available values are Donation, GiftInKind, MatchingGiftPledge, MatchingGiftPayment, PlannedGift, Pledge, PledgePayment, RecurringGift, RecurringGiftPayment, Stock, SoldStock, and Other.

receipt_status.description: Represents a list of gift receipt statuses. Returns gifts if their receipt status matches any specified. For example, "receipt_status=DoNotReceipt&receipt_status=Receipted" returns gifts that are marked either DoNotReceipt or Receipted.

acknowledgement_status.description: Represents a list of gift acknowledgement statuses. Returns gifts if their acknowledgement status matches any specified. For example, "acknowledgement_status=DoNotAcknowledge&acknowledgement_status=Acknowledged" returns gifts that are marked either DoNotAcknowledge or Acknowledged.

campaign_id.description: Represents a list of campaign identifiers. Returns gifts if any of the specified campaign identifiers match any of their campaigns. For example, "campaign_id=280&campaign_id=1232" returns gifts with either "280" or "1232" campaign identifiers.

fund_id.description: Represents a list of fund identifiers. Returns gifts if any of the specified fund identifiers match any of their funds. For example, "fund_id=280&fund_id=1232" returns gifts with either "280" or "1232" fund identifiers.

appeal_id.description: Represents a list of appeal identifiers. Returns gifts if any of the specified appeal identifiers match any of their appeals. For example, "appeal_id=280&appeal_id=1232" returns gifts with either "280" or "1232" appeal identifiers.

start_gift_date.description: Represents a filter for gifts with a gift date on or after the specified date. If used with end_gift_date, returns gifts with gift dates between both values.

end_gift_date.description: Represents a filter for gifts with a gift date on or before the specified date. If used with start_gift_date, returns gifts with gift dates between both values.

start_gift_amount.description: Represents a filter for gifts with an amount greater than or equal to the specified amount. If used with end_gift_amount, returns gifts with amounts between both values.

end_gift_amount.description: Represents a filter for gifts with an amount less than or equal to the specified amount. If used with start_gift_amount, returns gifts with amounts between both values.

list_id.description: Defines a list identifier used to filter the set of gifts to those included in the specified list. If this value is set, other specified filters will be ignored.

sort.description:
Represents a list of fields to sort the results by. Returns a list that sorts gifts based on the supplied fields. Results are in ascending order by default, and a '-' sign denotes descending order. For example, "sort=date_added,-date" sorts gifts by the "date_added" field in ascending order and then by the "gift date" field in descending order.
If only the date_modified field or only the date_added field is provided, then this adds the sort_token parameter to the next_link URL to ensure that gifts are stably sorted.

limit.description: Represents the number of records to return. The default is 500. The maximum is 5000.

offset.description: Represents the number of records to skip. For use with pagination.

GiftListOptions.description: Defines options and filter criteria when getting a list of gifts.

count.description: The number of items available for retrieval into the collection after applying any request parameters. The limit and offset parameters do not affect the count, but to facilitate paging, they may affect the number of items in the value result set.

next_link.description: For paginated responses, the URI for the next page of results.

value.description: The set of items included in the response. This may be a subset of the items in the collection.

ApiCollection_GiftRead_.description: Fetching data all at once quickly becomes unmanageable. The collection entity helps by returning paginated chunks of large data sets. This entity includes helpful metadata and properties to paginate and iterate through the data.

is_manual.description:
Indicates whether the gift is a manual gift.
If set to true, credit card and direct debit payments with transaction details will not charge. Recurring gifts that are manual will not have automated recurring gift payments generated. 
If no value is provided, the value will default to false.

acknowledgements.description: The acknowledgement associated with the gift. Array length must be less than or equal to 1. If none are provided, an empty acknowledgement with a status of NEEDSACKNOWLEDGEMENT will be created alongside the gift.

batch_number.description: Gets or sets the batch number. Character limit: 50 (including the batch prefix).

batch_prefix.description: Gets or sets the batch prefix. This must include at least one letter. It is required when BatchNumber has a value and defaults to "API" if no value is provided.

constituency.description: The constituency value of the gift. If no value is provided, the default constituency of the donor will be used.

constituent_id.description: The immutable system record ID of the constituent associated with the gift.

date.description: The gift date. Includes an offset from UTC in ISO-8601 format: 1969-11-21T10:29:43.

default_fundraiser_credits.description:
Indicates whether to use default fundraiser credits.
If no value is provided, defaults will not be used.

default_soft_credits.description:
Indicates whether to use default soft credits.
If no value is provided, defaults will not be used.

fundraisers.description: The set of fundraisers who receive credit for the gift.

gift_code.description: The gift code. Available values are the entries in the Gift Code table.

gift_splits.description: The set of gift splits associated with the gift.

gift_status.description: The status of the gift. Available values are Active, Held, Terminated, Completed, and Cancelled.

type.description: The gift type. Available values are Donation, Other, GiftInKind, RecurringGift, and RecurringGiftPayment.

is_anonymous.description:
Indicates whether the gift is anonymous.
If no value is provided, the default anonymity of the donor will be used.

linked_gifts.description: The recurring gift associated with the payment being added. When adding a recurring gift payment, a linked_gifts field must be included as an array of strings with the ID of the recurring gift to which the payment is linked. Array length must be less than or equal to 1.

lookup_id.description: The user-defined identifier for the gift. Character limit: 50. Lookup IDs for gifts created from an approved batch are automatically generated based on configuration settings in the database view.

origin.description:
The origin of the gift.
When provided, it must parse to a valid JSON object with one string field called "name". Additional fields may be added as desired.

payments.description: The payment on the gift. Array length must be less than or equal to 1.

post_date.description: The date that the gift was posted to general ledger. Includes an offset from UTC in ISO-8601 format: 1969-11-21T10:29:43.

post_status.description: The general ledger post status of the gift. Available values are Posted, NotPosted, and DoNotPost. When post_status is set to DoNotPost, post_date should be null. When it is set to NotPosted, post_date is required but remains editable. When it is set to Posted, post_date is required and is no longer editable. If no value is provided, a default value of NotPosted will be used.

receipts.description: The receipt associated with the gift. Array length must be less than or equal to 1. If none are provided, an empty receipt with a status of NEEDSRECEIPT will be added alongside the gift.

reference.description: Notes to track special details about a gift such as the motivation behind it or a detailed description of a gift-in-kind. Character limit: 255.

soft_credits.description: The set of soft credits associated with the gift.

subtype.description: The subtype of the gift. Available values are the entries in the Gift Subtypes table.

custom_fields.description: The set of custom fields associated with the gift.

GiftAdd.description: Gifts are the primary goal of fundraising efforts. They come in many forms and have a lot of information associated with them to ensure that they are properly allocated and acknowledged.
GiftAdd.x-additional-info:
Available values for the type of a gift:

Type Description
Amendment An amendment.
Donation A donation.
GiftInKind A gift-in-kind.
GeneralLedgerReversal A general ledger reversal.
MatchingGiftPledge A matching gift pledge.
MatchingGiftPayment A matching gift payment.
MatchingGiftWriteOff A matching gift write-off.
PlannedGift A planned gift.
Pledge A pledge.
PledgePayment A pledge payment.
PledgeWriteOff A pledge write off.
RecurringGift A recurring gift.
RecurringGiftPayment A recurring gift payment.
Stock A stock.
SoldStock A sold stock.
Other Another type of gift.

date.description: The date associated with the acknowledgement. Uses ISO-8601 format: 1969-11-21T10:29:43.

letter.description: The letter associated with the acknowledgement.

status.description: The status of the acknowledgement. Available values are: ACKNOWLEDGED, NEEDSACKNOWLEDGEMENT, and DONOTACKNOWLEDGE. When acknowledgement_status is set to DONOTACKNOWLEDGE, acknowledgement_letter and acknowledgement_date should be null. When it is set to NEEDSACKNOWLEDGEMENT, acknowledgement_date should be null. If no value is provided, a default value of NEEDSACKNOWLEDGEMENT will be used.

AcknowledgementAdd.description: Acknowledgement letters foster relationships with donors and show appreciation for their contributions. It is important to keep track of the acknowledgement status of gifts to ensure that each one gets a well-deserved thank you.

constituent_id.description: The immutable constituent system record ID for the fundraiser associated with the gift.

GiftFundraiserAdd.description: Fundraiser constituents interact with other constituents and prospects on behalf of your organization to cultivate relationships and request donations. When a constituent makes a donation, the fundraiser can receive credit to track performance and foster fundraising accomplishments.

appeal_id.description: The immutable system record ID of the appeal associated with the gift split.

campaign_id.description: The immutable system record ID of the campaign associated with the gift split.

fund_id.description: The immutable system record ID of the fund associated with the gift split.

package_id.description: The immutable system record ID of the package associated with the gift split.

GiftSplitAdd.description: Not all gifts serve a single purpose. Gift splits track donor wishes and allow you to divide gifts among multiple campaigns, funds, and appeals.

account_token.description: The account token. Must parse to a valid, non-empty GUID. Only applies to payment methods of CreditCard and DirectDebit. When adding a recurring gift, an account token must be provided for each payments object.

bbps_configuration_id.description: The bbps configuration ID. Must parse to a valid, non-empty GUID. Only applies to payment methods of CreditCard and DirectDebit.

bbps_transaction_id.description: The bbps transaction ID. Must parse to a valid, non-empty GUID. Only applies to payment methods of CreditCard and DirectDebit. When adding a recurring gift payment, either a bbps_transaction_id or checkout_transaction_id must be provided for each payments object.

check_number.description: The check number. Only applies to payment method of PersonalCheck. Character limit: 20.

checkout_transaction_id.description: The checkout transaction ID. Must parse to a valid, non-empty GUID. Only applies to payment methods of CreditCard and DirectDebit. When adding a recurring gift payment, either a bbps_transaction_id or checkout_transaction_id must be provided for each payments object.

charge_transaction.description: Whether or not to charge the checkout transaction. Only applies when a "CheckoutTransactionId" is supplied.

payment_method.description: The payment method. Available values are listed below. For donations, the payment method can be Cash, CreditCard, PersonalCheck, DirectDebit, Other, PayPal, or Venmo. For recurring gifts and recurring gift payments, the payment method must be CreditCard or DirectDebit.

reason.description: Optional. The Reason Code for this payment. Available values are Approved, HardReject, Held, and SoftReject.

reference.description: The reference. Only applies to payment method of Other.

rejection_details.description: The detail for why a payment was rejected. Generally comes from the gateway. This value will be ignored if reason is not set to HardReject, Held, or SoftReject.

PaymentAdd.description: Donors can use a variety of methods — such as Cash, Personal check, Credit card, and Direct debit — to pay for gifts.
PaymentAdd.x-additional-info:
Available values for the payment_method of a payment:

Type Description
Cash Cash.
PersonalCheck A personal check.
CreditCard A credit card.
StandingOrder A standing order.
DirectDebit Direct debit.
Voucher A voucher.
Other Another method of payment.
PayPal PayPal
Venmo Venmo

Note: For the ListGifts endpoint, only payment_method is returned.

date.description: The date that the gift was receipted. Includes an offset from UTC in ISO-8601 format: 1969-11-21T10:29:43.

status.description: The receipt status of the gift. Available values are RECEIPTED, NEEDSRECEIPT, and DONOTRECEIPT. When receipt_status is set to DONOTRECEIPT or NEEDSRECEIPT, receipt_date should be null. When it is set to RECEIPTED, receipt_date is required and is no longer editable. If no value is provided, a default value of NEEDSRECEIPT will be used.

receipt_stack.description: The receipt stack associated with the gift. Only available for customers using receipt stacks.

ReceiptAdd.description:
An object that represents the gift receipt to create.
To help donors track their giving for tax purposes, your organization should provide gift receipts.

end_date.description: Date the recurring gift should end. Uses ISO-8601 format: 1969-11-21T10:29:43.

frequency.description: Installment frequency of the recurring gift to add. Available values are WEEKLY, EVERY_TWO_WEEKS, EVERY_FOUR_WEEKS, MONTHLY, QUARTERLY, ANNUALLY.

start_date.description: Date the recurring gift should start. Uses ISO-8601 format: 1969-11-21T10:29:43.

RecurringGiftScheduleAdd.description: Defines a recurring gift schedule to add

constituent_id.description: The immutable system record ID of the constituent associated with the soft credit.

SoftCreditAdd.description: Soft credits allow you to recognize and track the indirect contributions of constituents such as spouses who influence the gifts of other constituents.

date.description: The date on the receipt. Uses ISO-8601 format: 1969-11-21T10:29:43.

status.description: The receipt status of the gift. Available values are: RECEIPTED, NEEDSRECEIPT, and DONOTRECEIPT. When receipt_status is set to NEEDSRECEIPT or DONOTRECEIPT, receipt_date should be null.

ReceiptEdit.description:
An object that represents the gift receipt to edit.
To help donors track their giving for tax purposes, your organization should provide gift receipts.

count.description: The number of items available for retrieval into the collection after applying any request parameters. The limit and offset parameters do not affect the count, but to facilitate paging, they may affect the number of items in the value result set.

next_link.description: For paginated responses, the URI for the next page of results.

value.description: The set of items included in the response. This may be a subset of the items in the collection.

ApiCollection_AttachmentRead_.description: Fetching data all at once quickly becomes unmanageable. The collection entity helps by returning paginated chunks of large data sets. This entity includes helpful metadata and properties to paginate and iterate through the data.

id.description: The immutable system record ID of the attachment.

content_type.description: The content type. For physical attachments only.

date.description: The date of the attachment. Uses ISO-8601 format:1969-11-21T10:29:43-04:00.

file_name.description: The file name. For physical attachments only.

file_size.description: The file size in bytes. For physical attachments only.

parent_id.description: The parent object's immutable system record ID.

tags.description: The tags associated with the attachment. Tags supplement the attachment’s name and description and identify it based on how an organization categorizes attachments. Available values are the entries in the Document Tags table.

thumbnail_id.description: The identifier of the thumbnail. For physical attachments that are images only.

thumbnail_url.description: The URL for a thumbnail. For physical attachments that are images only. Contains a time-bound signature that limits access to 30 minutes.

type.description: The attachment type. Available values are Link and Physical. Physical attachments are uploaded files such as images, PDFs, or Word documents that are saved locally or on the network. They are stored and managed in the system. Link attachments are links to files such as images, blog posts, or YouTube videos that are online or in a cloud storage account. They are stored and managed externally.

url.description: The URL for the attachment. The URL for a physical attachment contains a time-bound signature that limits access to 30 minutes.

AttachmentRead.description: Many cultivation activities collect related collateral, such as correspondence and prospect research. Attachments can save this data on the appropriate record to maintain a complete view of those activities.

date.description: The date of the attachment. Uses ISO-8601 format:1969-11-21T10:29:43-04:00. This property defaults to the current date and time if not supplied.

file_id.description: The identifier of the file. Character limit: 36. For physical attachments only.

file_name.description: The name of the file. Character limit: 255. For physical attachments only.

name.description: The name of the attachment. Character limit: 150.

parent_id.description: The parent object's immutable system record ID.

tags.description: The tags associated with the attachment. Tags supplement the attachment’s name and description and identify it based on how an organization categorizes attachments. Available values are the entries in the Document Tags table. Character limit: 100.

thumbnail_id.description: The identifier of the thumbnail. Character limit: 36. For physical attachments only.

type.description: The attachment type. Available values are Link and Physical. Physical attachments are uploaded files such as images, PDFs, or Word documents that are saved locally or on the network. They are stored and managed in the system. Link attachments are links to files such as images, blog posts, or YouTube videos that are online or in a cloud storage account. They are stored and managed externally.

url.description: The URL for the attachment. This is required for link attachments but does not apply to physical attachments.

AttachmentAdd.description: Many cultivation activities collect related collateral, such as correspondence and prospect research. Attachments can save this data on the appropriate record to maintain a complete view of those activities.

date.description: The date of the attachment. Uses ISO-8601 format:1969-11-21T10:29:43-04:00. This property cannot be set to null.

name.description: The name of the attachment. Character limit: 150.

tags.description: The tags associated with the attachment. Tags supplement the attachment’s name and description and identify it based on how an organization categorizes attachments. Available values are the entries in the Document Tags table. Character limit: 100.

url.description: The URL for the attachment. You can only edit the URL for link attachments. This property cannot be set to null.

AttachmentEdit.description: Many cultivation activities collect related collateral, such as correspondence and prospect research. Attachments can save this data on the appropriate record to maintain a complete view of those activities.

file_name.description: The file name. For physical attachments only.

upload_thumbnail.description: Indicates that a thumbnail needs to be created.

NewDocumentInfo.description: Cultivation activities often result in physical collateral such as images, PDFs, or Word files. The New Document entity allows you to upload these files to maintain a holistic view of target constituents.

FileDefinition.description: Contains metadata for uploading a document and unique document identifier for physical attachments.

headers.description: The headers to supply when making the request.

method.description: The http method to use for the request.

url.description: The url to use for the request.

RequestMetaData.description: The RequestMetadata entity specifies metadata for requests to upload physical attachments, including the URL for files, headers and the HTTP method to use.

Header.description: Request header used for uploading the physical attachment.

date.description: The date associated with the acknowledgement. Uses ISO-8601 format: 1969-11-21T10:29:43.

letter.description: The letter associated with the acknowledgement.

status.description: The status of the acknowledgement. Available values are: ACKNOWLEDGED, NEEDSACKNOWLEDGEMENT, and DONOTACKNOWLEDGE. When acknowledgement_status is set to DONOTACKNOWLEDGE, acknowledgement_letter and acknowledgement_date should be null. When it is set to NEEDSACKNOWLEDGEMENT, acknowledgement_date should be null.

AcknowledgementEdit.description: Acknowledgement letters foster relationships with donors and show appreciation for their contributions. It is important to keep track of the acknowledgement status of gifts to ensure that each one gets a well-deserved thank you.

tributes.description: A collection representing existing tribute records to add with the gift.

acknowledgements.description: The acknowledgement associated with the gift. Array length must be less than or equal to 1. If none are provided, an empty acknowledgement with a status of NEEDSACKNOWLEDGEMENT will be created alongside the gift.

batch_number.description: Gets or sets the batch number. Character limit: 50 (including the batch prefix).

batch_prefix.description: Gets or sets the batch prefix. This must include at least one letter. It is required when BatchNumber has a value and defaults to "API" if no value is provided.

constituency.description: The constituency value of the gift. If no value is provided, the default constituency of the donor will be used.

constituent_id.description: The immutable system record ID of the constituent associated with the gift.

date.description: The gift date. Includes an offset from UTC in ISO-8601 format: 1969-11-21T10:29:43.

default_fundraiser_credits.description:
Indicates whether to use default fundraiser credits.
If no value is provided, defaults will not be used.

default_soft_credits.description:
Indicates whether to use default soft credits.
If no value is provided, defaults will not be used.

fundraisers.description: The set of fundraisers who receive credit for the gift.

gift_code.description: The gift code. Available values are the entries in the Gift Code table.

gift_splits.description: The set of gift splits associated with the gift.

gift_status.description: The status of the gift. Available values are Active, Held, Terminated, Completed, and Cancelled.

type.description: The gift type. Available values are Donation, Other, GiftInKind, RecurringGift, and RecurringGiftPayment.

is_anonymous.description:
Indicates whether the gift is anonymous.
If no value is provided, the default anonymity of the donor will be used.

linked_gifts.description: The recurring gift associated with the payment being added. When adding a recurring gift payment, a linked_gifts field must be included as an array of strings with the ID of the recurring gift to which the payment is linked. Array length must be less than or equal to 1.

lookup_id.description: The user-defined identifier for the gift. Character limit: 50. Lookup IDs for gifts created from an approved batch are automatically generated based on configuration settings in the database view.

origin.description:
The origin of the gift.
When provided, it must parse to a valid JSON object with one string field called "name". Additional fields may be added as desired.

payments.description: The payment on the gift. Array length must be less than or equal to 1.

post_date.description: The date that the gift was posted to general ledger. Includes an offset from UTC in ISO-8601 format: 1969-11-21T10:29:43.

post_status.description: The general ledger post status of the gift. Available values are Posted, NotPosted, and DoNotPost. When post_status is set to DoNotPost, post_date should be null. When it is set to NotPosted, post_date is required but remains editable. When it is set to Posted, post_date is required and is no longer editable. If no value is provided, a default value of NotPosted will be used.

receipts.description: The receipt associated with the gift. Array length must be less than or equal to 1. If none are provided, an empty receipt with a status of NEEDSRECEIPT will be added alongside the gift.

reference.description: Notes to track special details about a gift such as the motivation behind it or a detailed description of a gift-in-kind. Character limit: 255.

soft_credits.description: The set of soft credits associated with the gift.

subtype.description: The subtype of the gift. Available values are the entries in the Gift Subtypes table.

custom_fields.description: The set of custom fields associated with the gift.

GiftBatchGiftAdd.description: Gifts are the primary goal of fundraising efforts. They come in many forms and have a lot of information associated with them to ensure that they are properly allocated and acknowledged.
GiftBatchGiftAdd.x-additional-info:
Available values for the type of a gift:

Type Description
Amendment An amendment.
Donation A donation.
GiftInKind A gift-in-kind.
GeneralLedgerReversal A general ledger reversal.
MatchingGiftPledge A matching gift pledge.
MatchingGiftPayment A matching gift payment.
MatchingGiftWriteOff A matching gift write-off.
PlannedGift A planned gift.
Pledge A pledge.
PledgePayment A pledge payment.
PledgeWriteOff A pledge write off.
RecurringGift A recurring gift.
RecurringGiftPayment A recurring gift payment.
Stock A stock.
SoldStock A sold stock.
Other Another type of gift.

id.description: The system record ID of the tribute.

tribute_acknowledgees.description: The acknowledgees for this tribute record to add with a gift tribute.

TributeAdd.description: An object representing an existing tribute record to add with a gift.

id.description: The system record ID of the tribute acknowledgee.

TributeAcknowledgeeAdd.description: An object representing an existing tribute acknowledgee record to add with a gift tribute.

errors.description: The batch gift errors associated with the batch gift add operation

gifts.description: The collection of batch gifts that were added by the batch gift add operation

BatchGiftAddResults.description: Contains a collection of batch gift error records and the batch gifts that the operation added

affected_field.description: The field affected by the error

batch_id.description: The immutable system record ID of the batch

gift_id.description: The immutable system record ID of the gift

lookup_id.description: The user-defined identifier for the gift.

batch_id.description: The ID of the batch to which the gift was added.

approved_gift_id.description: The ID of the gift to which the batch gift was approved.

tributes.description: The collection of tributes added to the batch gift.

errors.description: The errors associated with the batch gift.

id.description: The immutable system record ID of the gift.

acknowledgements.description: The set of acknowledgements associated with the gift.

batch_number.description: The batch number associated with this gift.

constituent_id.description: The immutable system record ID of the constituent associated with the gift.

date_added.description: The date when the gift was created. Includes an offset from UTC in ISO-8601 format: 1969-11-21T10:29:43-04:00.

date_modified.description: The date when the gift was last modified. Includes an offset from UTC in ISO-8601 format: 1969-11-21T10:29:43-04:00.

fundraisers.description: The set of fundraisers who receive credit for the gift.

gift_aid_qualification_status.description: This computed field determines the Gift Aid qualification status based on tax declaration information and the database format. Available values are: Qualified, NotQualified, and PartlyQualified. For the UK only.

gift_code.description: The gift code value associated with the gift.

gift_splits.description: The set of gift splits associated with the gift.

gift_status.description: The status of the gift. Available values are Active, Held, Terminated, Completed, and Cancelled.

is_anonymous.description: Indicates whether the gift is anonymous.

linked_gifts.description: The identifiers of other gifts that are linked to this gift.

lookup_id.description: The user-defined identifier for the gift.

post_date.description: The date that the gift was posted to general ledger. Uses ISO-8601 format: 1969-11-21T10:29:43.

post_status.description: The general ledger post status of the gift. Available values are Posted, NotPosted, and DoNotPost. When post_status is set to DoNotPost, post_date should be null. When it is set to NotPosted, post_date is required but remains editable. When it is set to Posted, post_date is required and is no longer editable.

receipts.description: The set of receipts associated with the gift.

reference.description: Notes to track special details about a gift such as the motivation behind it or a detailed description of a gift-in-kind.

soft_credits.description: The set of soft credits associated with the gift.

subtype.description: The subtype of the gift. Available values are the entries in the Gift Subtypes table.

type.description: The gift type. Available values are Donation, GiftInKind, Pledge, PledgePayment, RecurringGift, RecurringGiftPayment, Stock, SoldStock, Other, PlannedGift, MatchingGiftPledge, MatchingGiftPayment

BatchGiftRead.description: Gifts are the primary goal of fundraising efforts. They come in many forms and have a lot of information associated with them to ensure that they are properly allocated and acknowledged.
BatchGiftRead.x-additional-info:
Available values for the type of a gift:

Type Description
Amendment An amendment.
Donation A donation.
GiftInKind A gift-in-kind.
GeneralLedgerReversal A general ledger reversal.
MatchingGiftPledge A matching gift pledge.
MatchingGiftPayment A matching gift payment.
MatchingGiftWriteOff A matching gift write-off.
PlannedGift A planned gift.
Pledge A pledge.
PledgePayment A pledge payment.
PledgeWriteOff A pledge write off.
RecurringGift A recurring gift.
RecurringGiftPayment A recurring gift payment.
Stock A stock.
SoldStock A sold stock.
Other Another type of gift.

id.description: The system record ID of the tribute.

tribute_acknowledgees.description: The acknowledgees for this tribute record on the gift.

TributeRead.description: An object representing an existing tribute record on a gift.

id.description: The system record ID of the tribute acknowledgee.

TributeAcknowledgeeRead.description: An object representing an existing tribute acknowledgee record on a gift tribute.

can_be_converted.description: Indicates if the gift can be converted to an automated recurring gift.

token_will_be_required.description: Indicates if a token will be required to an automated recurring gift.

gift_id.description: The immutable system record ID of the gift.

errors.description: The collection of errors explaining why the gift cannot be converted to automated, if it cannot be.

RecurringGiftConversionCheck.description: The result of a recurring gift conversion check.  This indicates if a recurring gift can be converted to an automated recurring gift.

gift_id.description: The immutable system record ID of the gift.

lookup_id.description: The lookup ID of the gift.

affected_field.description: The field affected by the error.

RecurringGiftConversionError.description: An error when attempting to convert a recurring gift.  This indicates the specific reason that a recurring gift cannot be converted to automated.

bbps_configuration_id.description: BBPS Configuration Id to put on the gift

account_token.description: Account token to put on the gift payment

RecurringGiftConversionOptions.description: Options to configure conversion of a manual recurring gift to an automated recurring gift.

gift_status.description: The new status of the recurring gift. Available values are Active, Held, Terminated, Completed, and Cancelled.

RecurringGiftStatusEdit.description: An object that contains information needed to update the status of a recurring gift.

count.description: The number of items available for retrieval into the collection after applying any request parameters. The limit and offset parameters do not affect the count, but to facilitate paging, they may affect the number of items in the value result set.

next_link.description: For paginated responses, the URI for the next page of results.

value.description: The result set of items after applying any request parameters. To facilitate paging, this may be a subset of all available items.

ApiCollection.description: Fetching data all at once quickly becomes unmanageable. The collection entity helps by returning paginated chunks of large data sets. This entity includes helpful metadata and properties to paginate and iterate through the data.
